event: Cairo Agreement signed; PLO armed presence in Lebanon formally legitimized —establishes precedent of international/host-state endorsement ofnon-state armed presence
description: Cairo Agreement signed; PLO armed presence in Lebanon formally legitimized —establishes precedent of international/host-state endorsement ofnon-state armed presence

event: Cairo Agreement
description: The Cairo Agreement formally grants Palestinians the right to launch operations from Lebanese soil following clashes between Palestinian forces and the Lebanese army. It enables the growth of a Palestinian state-within-a-state in the south.

event: Black September:PLO expelled from Jordan, relocates headquarters to Lebanon
description: Black September:PLO expelled from Jordan, relocates headquarters to Lebanon

event: Black September – PLO expelled from Jordan
description: Civil war in Jordan results in the expulsion of PLO forces, accelerating their consolidation in southern Lebanon. This significantly increases cross-border tensions with Israel.

event: Ayn al-Rummana bus ambush by Phalangists: Lebanese Civil War begins
description: Ayn al-Rummana bus ambush by Phalangists: Lebanese Civil War begins

event: Fall of Tal al-Zat'ar after 53-day siege: approximately 3,000 killed; PLO position in Lebanon significantlyweakened
description: Fall of Tal al-Zat'ar after 53-day siege: approximately 3,000 killed; PLO position in Lebanon significantlyweakened

event: Israel completes formal withdrawal but transfers positions to SLA (Haddad forces), not UNIFIL — first major mandate failure
description: Israel completes formal withdrawal but transfers positions to SLA (Haddad forces), not UNIFIL — first major mandate failure

event: UNSCR 425/426: UNIFIL established following Israeli Litani Operation
description: UNSCR 425/426: UNIFIL established following Israeli Litani Operation

event: UNSCResolution 425 adopted; UNIFIL created with initial six-monthmandate
description: UNSCResolution 425 adopted; UNIFIL created with initial six-monthmandate

event: First UNIFIL troops deploy tosouth Lebanon
description: First UNIFIL troops deploy tosouth Lebanon

event: Israeli Operation Litani; UNSCR 425/426; UNIFIL established; Force Commanderfalsely certifies IDF withdrawal
description: Israeli Operation Litani; UNSCR 425/426; UNIFIL established; Force Commanderfalsely certifies IDF withdrawal

event: Israeli Litani Operation; UNIFILcreated; 1978 invasion strengthens Amal's position in the south at PLO'sexpense
description: Israeli Litani Operation; UNIFILcreated; 1978 invasion strengthens Amal's position in the south at PLO'sexpense

event: UNIFIL HQplaced in Israeli-controlled enclave at Naqura; supply lines run through Israel — structural compromise of mission independence
description: UNIFIL HQplaced in Israeli-controlled enclave at Naqura; supply lines run through Israel — structural compromise of mission independence

event: Operation Litani
description: Israel invades and occupies South Lebanon up to the Litani River in response to PLO cross-border attacks. The operation marks the beginning of prolonged Israeli military engagement in Lebanese territory.

event: UNSC Resolution 425 – UNIFIL established
description: The Security Council passes Resolution 425 (with abstentions from Czechoslovakia and the USSR), calling for Israeli withdrawal and establishing UNIFIL. The mission is mandated to confirm withdrawal, restore peace, and assist Lebanon in reasserting authority in the south.

event: First UNIFIL troops arrive
description: The first contingent of UNIFIL peacekeepers deploys to southern Lebanon just days after Resolution 425. The force begins operating in an area still contested between Israeli forces and armed Palestinian groups.

event: Israeli withdrawal – SLA handover
description: Israel withdraws its forces under US pressure but transfers the border zone to the right-wing South Lebanon Army (SLA) auxiliary. Israel is widely regarded as retaining effective control, leading to repeated renewal of UNIFIL's mandate.

event: Haddad militia kills two Irish UNIFIL soldiers;significant international outcry and diplomatic damage to Israeli-Irishrelations
description: Haddad militia kills two Irish UNIFIL soldiers;significant international outcry and diplomatic damage to Israeli-Irishrelations

event: PLO–Israel ceasefire (Habib)
description: Intensifying cross-border exchanges between the PLO and Israel prompt US Ambassador Philip Habib to broker a ceasefire. The agreement holds temporarily but is later used by Israel as a pretext for the 1982 invasion.

event: Second Israeli invasion ('Peace for Galilee'): UNIFIL overrun and sidelined; US vetoes UNSC expansion of mandate
description: Second Israeli invasion ('Peace for Galilee'): UNIFIL overrun and sidelined; US vetoes UNSC expansion of mandate

event: Israeli invasion reaches Beirut; UNIFIL unable to block advance; contingents attempt symbolic butineffective resistance
description: Israeli invasion reaches Beirut; UNIFIL unable to block advance; contingents attempt symbolic butineffective resistance

event: Israeli invasion 'Peace for Galilee'; siege of West Beirut; PLO evacuation under Habib Plan
description: Israeli invasion 'Peace for Galilee'; siege of West Beirut; PLO evacuation under Habib Plan

event: Sabra and Shatilla massacre:approximately 1,000+ killed by Phalangist forces with Israelifacilitation
description: Sabra and Shatilla massacre:approximately 1,000+ killed by Phalangist forces with Israelifacilitation

event: IDF advances through UNIFIL area of operations en routeto Beirut; UNIFIL ordered to stand down; no resistanceoffered
description: IDF advances through UNIFIL area of operations en routeto Beirut; UNIFIL ordered to stand down; no resistanceoffered

event: Operation Peace for Galilee
description: Following the wounding of Israeli Ambassador Argov in London by Abu Nidal gunmen (Iraqi-linked, not PLO), Israel launches its second invasion of Lebanon, advancing as far as Beirut. The invasion dramatically expands Israel's occupation and reshapes the Lebanese political landscape.

event: Israeli partial withdrawal from Beirut
description: Israel pulls back from the Beirut region but retains occupation of the entire south up to the Awwali River north of Sidon. Full occupation of the south continues for a further three years.

event: Israeli convoyenters Nabatiya during Ashura procession; triggers Shi'a resistance andclerical fatwa — identified by Göksel as a key turning point for armedopposition to the occupation
description: Israeli convoyenters Nabatiya during Ashura procession; triggers Shi'a resistance andclerical fatwa — identified by Göksel as a key turning point for armedopposition to the occupation

event: Ashura incident – fatwa for defensive jihad
description: Israeli forces violate the sanctity of Ashura, prompting senior Sunni and Shia clerics to issue a fatwa declaring a defensive jihad against the occupation. The incident radicalises previously moderate religious figures in the region.

event: Israel-Lebanon 'peace' accord
description: A US-brokered accord between Israel and Lebanon is signed but quickly collapses. Syria, whose consent was not sought, effectively vetoes the agreement.

event: Beirut barracks bombing
description: A Hezbollah-linked suicide bombing destroys the US Marine barracks in Beirut, killing over 240 Marines. The attack is one of the deadliest single-day losses for the US military since World War II.

event: Israeli cabinet approves stagedwithdrawal; residual 'security zone' established under SLA/Lahadcommand
description: Israeli cabinet approves stagedwithdrawal; residual 'security zone' established under SLA/Lahadcommand

event: IDF completes third-stage withdrawal; post-1985 security zone consolidated and expanded under SLA/Lahad
description: IDF completes third-stage withdrawal; post-1985 security zone consolidated and expanded under SLA/Lahad

event: Hezbollah Open Letter
description: Hezbollah publishes its founding ideological manifesto, the 'Open Letter,' formally articulating its goals, identity, and resistance posture. The document establishes the organisation's ideological framework for decades to come.

event: War of the Camps begins
description: Amal launches a siege of Palestinian refugee camps in Beirut and the south, initiating the so-called 'War of the Camps.' The conflict lasts approximately two years and deepens sectarian and factional divisions.

event: Israeli phased withdrawal – security belt retained
description: Israel completes a phased withdrawal from most of southern Lebanon but retains a 10–20 km 'security belt' along the border, including a salient around Jizzin — roughly 10% of Lebanese territory. The SLA continues to administer the zone under Israeli direction.

event: Kidnappingof Col. Higgins (UNTSO/UNIFIL liaison officer) — significant escalationwith impact on UNIFIL-LAF-Amaldynamics
description: Kidnappingof Col. Higgins (UNTSO/UNIFIL liaison officer) — significant escalationwith impact on UNIFIL-LAF-Amaldynamics

event: Amal–Hezbollah hostilities begin
description: Armed clashes break out between Amal and Hezbollah, reflecting growing competition for Shia political and military dominance in Lebanon. The conflict is eventually resolved by the 1990 Damascus Accord.

event: Kidnapping of Lt. Col. William Higgins
description: Lt. Col. William Higgins, serving with the UN Observer Group Lebanon, is kidnapped by the Amal splinter group 'Believer's Resistance' led by Mustafa Dirani, with suspected Hezbollah sympathies. Higgins is later killed; responsibility is attributed to the 'Revolutionary Justice Organization.'

event: Ta'if Agreement
description: The Ta'if Agreement ends Lebanon's civil war and mandates disarmament of all militias including Amal. Amal's entry into formal politics causes its militant cadres to migrate toward Hezbollah, strengthening the latter.

event: Damascus Accord – Amal–Hezbollah peace
description: Iran and Syria sponsor the Damascus Accord, ending two years of armed hostilities between Amal and Hezbollah. The agreement consolidates Hezbollah's position as the dominant Shia armed movement.

event: Hizballah participates in Lebaneseparliamentary elections for the first time, winning 12 seats — strategicpivot to political actor
description: Hizballah participates in Lebaneseparliamentary elections for the first time, winning 12 seats — strategicpivot to political actor

event: Operation Accountability:Israeli bombardment; approximately 147 Lebanese killed, 350,000 displaced; US-brokered ceasefire
description: Operation Accountability:Israeli bombardment; approximately 147 Lebanese killed, 350,000 displaced; US-brokered ceasefire

event: Operation Accountability: Israeli bombardment of south Lebanon; ~130 killed, 500 injured, ~200,000 displaced from UNIFIL area
description: Operation Accountability: Israeli bombardment of south Lebanon; ~130 killed, 500 injured, ~200,000 displaced from UNIFIL area

event: Operation Accountability
description: Israel launches Operation Accountability on 25 July, targeting Hezbollah bases north of the security zone with the aim of curtailing guerrilla operations. A US-brokered ceasefire is reached on 31 July through Secretary of State Warren Christopher.

event: Qana massacre: IDF shells UNIFIL Fijian HQ sheltering ~800 civilians; 106 killed including 52 children; Van Kappen investigation initiated but suppressed
description: Qana massacre: IDF shells UNIFIL Fijian HQ sheltering ~800 civilians; 106 killed including 52 children; Van Kappen investigation initiated but suppressed

event: Qana massacre: IDF shells UNIFIL Fijian HQ sheltering ~800 civilians; 106 killed including 52 children
description: Qana massacre: IDF shells UNIFIL Fijian HQ sheltering ~800 civilians; 106 killed including 52 children

event: Israel-Hezbollah Document of Understanding establishes informal rules of the game
description: Israel-Hezbollah Document of Understanding establishes informal rules of the game

event: IDF shellingkills 100+ civilians sheltering at Fijian UNIFIL HQ, Qana — Göksel waspresent during and after the incident
description: IDF shellingkills 100+ civilians sheltering at Fijian UNIFIL HQ, Qana — Göksel waspresent during and after the incident

event: Israeli Operation Grapes of Wrath; Qana massacre (approximately 100+ civilians killed at UNIFIL compound)
description: Israeli Operation Grapes of Wrath; Qana massacre (approximately 100+ civilians killed at UNIFIL compound)

event: Operation Grapes of Wrath
description: Israel conducts Operation Grapes of Wrath against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, the third major Israeli military campaign aimed at pressuring the Lebanese population against the group. The operation ends with a US-brokered understanding similar to the 1993 ceasefire.

event: Tufayli 'revolt of the hungry'
description: Former Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Subhi Tufayli launches a populist initiative demanding jobs, crop subsidies, free education, utilities, and state pensions for resistance fighters. The move represents a direct challenge to both Hezbollah's leadership and state authority.

event: Tufayli expelled from Hezbollah
description: After months of defiance, Tufayli attempts to disrupt Hezbollah's Jerusalem Day commemoration in Baalbek and is expelled from the organisation within 24 hours. The episode underscores Hezbollah's intolerance of internal dissent.

event: Israelwithdraws unilaterally from Security Zone; UNIFIL verifies Blue Linedemarcation
description: Israelwithdraws unilaterally from Security Zone; UNIFIL verifies Blue Linedemarcation

event: IDFunilateral withdrawal from south Lebanon; SLA collapses; Hizballahrapidly expands social infrastructure into formerly occupiedterritory
description: IDFunilateral withdrawal from south Lebanon; SLA collapses; Hizballahrapidly expands social infrastructure into formerly occupiedterritory

event: Three IDF soldiers kidnapped at Shab'a Farms — first major post-withdrawal test of the 'rules of thegame'
description: Three IDF soldiers kidnapped at Shab'a Farms — first major post-withdrawal test of the 'rules of thegame'

event: Israel withdraws from south Lebanon; SLA disbandsovernight; UNIFIL reduced to approximately 2,000 troops
description: Israel withdraws from south Lebanon; SLA disbandsovernight; UNIFIL reduced to approximately 2,000 troops

event: Lebanon refuses to participate informal UN Blue Line demarcation process — an early signal of the state'sambivalent attitude toward boundary formalization
description: Lebanon refuses to participate informal UN Blue Line demarcation process — an early signal of the state'sambivalent attitude toward boundary formalization

event: Israeli full withdrawal from south Lebanon
description: Israel withdraws completely from south Lebanon and the SLA is disbanded, ending 22 years of occupation. UNIFIL remains deployed as the Lebanese government has still not restored effective authority over the area.

event: First local elections in south Lebanon in 38 years; Amal-Hizballah electoral pact wins 90 of 115 municipal councils
description: First local elections in south Lebanon in 38 years; Amal-Hizballah electoral pact wins 90 of 115 municipal councils

event: UN scales down UNIFIL; Finland and Ireland withdraw
description: UN scales down UNIFIL; Finland and Ireland withdraw

event: Mt. Dov escalation;Iranian FM Kharazi urges Hizballah restraint in Beirut — documents Iranian dual restraint role
description: Mt. Dov escalation;Iranian FM Kharazi urges Hizballah restraint in Beirut — documents Iranian dual restraint role

event: IAF strikes Syrian territory (Palestinian camp near Damascus) — first such strike in 30 years; tests 'rules of the game'threshold
description: IAF strikes Syrian territory (Palestinian camp near Damascus) — first such strike in 30 years; tests 'rules of the game'threshold

event: UNSC Resolution 1559
description: The Security Council passes Resolution 1559, calling for the withdrawal of all foreign forces from Lebanon and the dismantling of all militias. The resolution is widely understood as targeting both Syrian presence and Hezbollah's armed wing.

event: Syrian military withdrawal from Lebanon
description: Syria completes its military withdrawal from Lebanon following massive domestic and international pressure in the wake of the Hariri assassination. The withdrawal ends nearly three decades of Syrian military presence.

event: UNSC Resolution 1701 adopted; UNIFIL II authorised up to 15,000 troops
description: UNSC Resolution 1701 adopted; UNIFIL II authorised up to 15,000 troops

event: Hezbollahcross-border raid kills 9 Israeli soldiers, kidnaps 2; triggers 2006 warand eventual SCR 1701 expansion of UNIFIL
description: Hezbollahcross-border raid kills 9 Israeli soldiers, kidnaps 2; triggers 2006 warand eventual SCR 1701 expansion of UNIFIL

event: Italy hosts Rome peace conference; lays diplomatic groundwork for Resolution 1701
description: Italy hosts Rome peace conference; lays diplomatic groundwork for Resolution 1701

event: Italy provides interim naval vessel to enable Israeli sea blockade lift; Interim MTF formed
description: Italy provides interim naval vessel to enable Israeli sea blockade lift; Interim MTF formed

event: UNSC Resolution 1701 adopted; UNIFIL reinforced to up to 15,000 troops with European TCC backbone
description: UNSC Resolution 1701 adopted; UNIFIL reinforced to up to 15,000 troops with European TCC backbone

event: Finland and Ireland return to UNIFIL at UN request as joint Finnish/Irish Battalion
description: Finland and Ireland return to UNIFIL at UN request as joint Finnish/Irish Battalion

event: Rome peace conference – Italy signals troop commitment
description: Newly elected Italian PM Romano Prodi co-chairs a peace conference in Rome with the US, calling for an ad-hoc peacekeeping mission in Lebanon and signalling Italy's readiness to contribute troops. The conference foreshadows the expanded UNIFIL mandate under Resolution 1701.

event: July 2006 Israel-Lebanon War
description: Israel launches a major military campaign against Lebanon primarily targeting Hezbollah, following the capture of two Israeli soldiers. The war results in UNSC Resolution 1701 and a significantly expanded UNIFIL mandate.

event: SCR 1701 adopted, ending Second Lebanon War; UNIFIL II mandate expansion authorized
description: SCR 1701 adopted, ending Second Lebanon War; UNIFIL II mandate expansion authorized

event: Spain conducts 645 QIPs covering 54 villages and 99,000 residents in Marjayoun district
description: Spain conducts 645 QIPs covering 54 villages and 99,000 residents in Marjayoun district

event: Italian General Graziano assumes UNIFIL Force Command (until Jan 2010)
description: Italian General Graziano assumes UNIFIL Force Command (until Jan 2010)

event: Spanish UNIFIL peacekeepers killed near al-Khiyam
description: Six soldiers of the Spanish UNIFIL battalion are killed by a vehicle-borne IED near the Shia village of al-Khiyam. The attack is one of the deadliest against UNIFIL since the mission's expansion in 2006.

event: Six Spanish UNIFIL soldiers killed by VBIED near al-Khiyam — deadliest single attack on UNIFIL II
description: Six Spanish UNIFIL soldiers killed by VBIED near al-Khiyam — deadliest single attack on UNIFIL II

event: Israeli-Hezbollah prisoner exchange mediated by Germandiplomat Gerhard Conrad — significant German bilateralcontribution
description: Israeli-Hezbollah prisoner exchange mediated by Germandiplomat Gerhard Conrad — significant German bilateralcontribution

event: Lebanese National Dialogue under President Suleiman
description: President Michel Suleiman convenes a national dialogue process addressing core sovereignty and disarmament questions, including Hezbollah's weapons. The dialogue reflects ongoing Lebanese political paralysis over the resistance arms issue.

event: LAF-IDF clash near Blue Line; UNIFIL Indonesian contingent unable to de-escalate
description: LAF-IDF clash near Blue Line; UNIFIL Indonesian contingent unable to de-escalate

event: LAF, coordinating with UNIFIL, declares Blue Line area aclosed military zone during Nakba Day protests; prevents cross-bordercivilian incursion
description: LAF, coordinating with UNIFIL, declares Blue Line area aclosed military zone during Nakba Day protests; prevents cross-bordercivilian incursion

event: EU blacklists Hezbollah military wing; Italian FM Bonino publicly opposes; UNIFIL community relations strained
description: EU blacklists Hezbollah military wing; Italian FM Bonino publicly opposes; UNIFIL community relations strained

event: EU blacklists Hezbollah military wing
description: The European Union designates Hezbollah's military wing as a terrorist organisation, following years of debate among member states. The designation complicates UNIFIL's liaison arrangements with Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, given that several major TCCs are EU members.

event: Estonia joins Finnish contingent as platoon-sized unit in UNIFIL Western Sector
description: Estonia joins Finnish contingent as platoon-sized unit in UNIFIL Western Sector

event: Hezbollah ATGM attack kills two IDF soldiers near Shebaa Farms; Israeli return fire kills one Spanish UNIFIL member
description: Hezbollah ATGM attack kills two IDF soldiers near Shebaa Farms; Israeli return fire kills one Spanish UNIFIL member

event: SCR 2373 adopted; demands more prompt and detailedreporting on 1701 violations from UNIFIL
description: SCR 2373 adopted; demands more prompt and detailedreporting on 1701 violations from UNIFIL

event: Hezbollahfighters deliberately photographed near UNIFIL headquarters during ascheduled Blue Line liaison tour — documented information operationagainst both UNIFIL and LAF
description: Hezbollahfighters deliberately photographed near UNIFIL headquarters during ascheduled Blue Line liaison tour — documented information operationagainst both UNIFIL and LAF

event: Hezbollah launches unilateral military operation against Jabhat al-Nusra in Arsal border area; LAF adopts strict deconflictionposture; ceasefire declared 27 July
description: Hezbollah launches unilateral military operation against Jabhat al-Nusra in Arsal border area; LAF adopts strict deconflictionposture; ceasefire declared 27 July

event: First UNIFIL periodic report following SCR 2373 confirmsno 'verified findings' at any suspected Hezbollah sites despite enhancedmonitoring requirements
description: First UNIFIL periodic report following SCR 2373 confirmsno 'verified findings' at any suspected Hezbollah sites despite enhancedmonitoring requirements

event: President Aoun publicly declares Hezbollah weapons 'a principal element of Lebanon's defense' — formally collapses state/non-state distinction underpinning SCR 1701
description: President Aoun publicly declares Hezbollah weapons 'a principal element of Lebanon's defense' — formally collapses state/non-state distinction underpinning SCR 1701

event: President Aoun publicly defends Hezbollah's right to maintain weapons as Lebanon's strategic deterrent — formally legitimizes violation of SCR 1701 disarmament provisions
description: President Aoun publicly defends Hezbollah's right to maintain weapons as Lebanon's strategic deterrent — formally legitimizes violation of SCR 1701 disarmament provisions

event: UNIFIL patrol attacked near Majdal Zoun by approximately 20 armed activists; Lebanese investigation contradicts UN findings; perpetrators unprosecuted as of 2019
description: UNIFIL patrol attacked near Majdal Zoun by approximately 20 armed activists; Lebanese investigation contradicts UN findings; perpetrators unprosecuted as of 2019

event: IDF Operation Northern Shield begins; exposes six Hezbollah cross-border attack tunnels crossing the Blue Line into Israel; concludes 13 Jan 2019
description: IDF Operation Northern Shield begins; exposes six Hezbollah cross-border attack tunnels crossing the Blue Line into Israel; concludes 13 Jan 2019

event: UN observers witness concrete flowing from a Lebanese chicken coop into a tunnel crossing the Blue Line — first irrefutable physical proof of tunnel infrastructure
description: UN observers witness concrete flowing from a Lebanese chicken coop into a tunnel crossing the Blue Line — first irrefutable physical proof of tunnel infrastructure

event: UNIFIL-LAF MOU signed on de-mining south of Litani
description: UNIFIL-LAF MOU signed on de-mining south of Litani

event: Launch of formal Lebanon-Israel maritime bordernegotiations under US mediation
description: Launch of formal Lebanon-Israel maritime bordernegotiations under US mediation

event: Beirut port explosion: 218 killed, 7,000+ injured; fundamentally redirects international attention from southern Lebanon security
description: Beirut port explosion: 218 killed, 7,000+ injured; fundamentally redirects international attention from southern Lebanon security

event: SCR 2539 adopted; UNIFIL force ceiling reduced from 15,000 to 13,000; mandate renewed for one year without substantive change
description: SCR 2539 adopted; UNIFIL force ceiling reduced from 15,000 to 13,000; mandate renewed for one year without substantive change

event: Spanish General assumes command of UNIFIL mission
description: Spanish General assumes command of UNIFIL mission

event: Hezbollah opens fire across Blue Line in solidarity with Gaza; south Lebanon war resumes
description: Hezbollah opens fire across Blue Line in solidarity with Gaza; south Lebanon war resumes

event: Ceasefire enters into force; frontline villages remain under Israeli occupation blocking civilian return
description: Ceasefire enters into force; frontline villages remain under Israeli occupation blocking civilian return

event: Israeli escalation; assassination of Nasrallah and Hezbollah leadership; ground invasion of south Lebanon
description: Israeli escalation; assassination of Nasrallah and Hezbollah leadership; ground invasion of south Lebanon

event: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire; Quintet monitoring mechanismestablished replacing Trilateral Military Committee
description: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire; Quintet monitoring mechanismestablished replacing Trilateral Military Committee

event: Fall of Assad regime; Hezbollah loses Syrian overlandresupply route to Iran — significant strategic setback
description: Fall of Assad regime; Hezbollah loses Syrian overlandresupply route to Iran — significant strategic setback

event: Israel-Hezbollah war; UNIFIL confined to bases; IDF-UNIFIL serious friction; Hezbollah infrastructure throughout AO fully exposed
description: Israel-Hezbollah war; UNIFIL confined to bases; IDF-UNIFIL serious friction; Hezbollah infrastructure throughout AO fully exposed

event: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire; Quintet Enforcement Committee (US, France, Israel, Lebanon, UNIFIL) established — replaces Trilateral Military Committee
description: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire; Quintet Enforcement Committee (US, France, Israel, Lebanon, UNIFIL) established — replaces Trilateral Military Committee

event: Israeli forces still occupy south Lebanon blocking civilian return; agricultural land destruction continues
description: Israeli forces still occupy south Lebanon blocking civilian return; agricultural land destruction continues

event: SCR 2790 adopted; final UNIFIL mandate renewal to 31 December 2026 — driven primarily by US pressure
description: SCR 2790 adopted; final UNIFIL mandate renewal to 31 December 2026 — driven primarily by US pressure

event: 12-day US-Israeli war on Iran; Hezbollah stays out,preserving its forces but taking a significant legitimacy hitdomestically
description: 12-day US-Israeli war on Iran; Hezbollah stays out,preserving its forces but taking a significant legitimacy hitdomestically

event: UNSCR 2790 adopted: final UNIFIL mandate extension to 31 December 2026; closure confirmed
description: UNSCR 2790 adopted: final UNIFIL mandate extension to 31 December 2026; closure confirmed

event: Hezbollah fires approximately 200 rockets coordinatedwith Iranian missile barrage; Israel applies 'Gaza model': massdisplacement, razing of border villages, deep buffer zone to Litani
description: Hezbollah fires approximately 200 rockets coordinatedwith Iranian missile barrage; Israel applies 'Gaza model': massdisplacement, razing of border villages, deep buffer zone to Litani

event: Hostilities resume between Israel and Hezbollah following assassination of Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei
description: Hostilities resume between Israel and Hezbollah following assassination of Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei

event: US-Israeli war on Iranresumes and escalates significantly
description: US-Israeli war on Iranresumes and escalates significantly

event: Assassination of Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei;Hezbollah fires on Israel in response; Lebanese government issuesunprecedented formal ban on Hezbollah military activity
description: Assassination of Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei;Hezbollah fires on Israel in response; Lebanese government issuesunprecedented formal ban on Hezbollah military activity

event: Quintet monitoring mechanism collapses; Israel stopsattending meetings; no functional de-escalation mechanismremains
description: Quintet monitoring mechanism collapses; Israel stopsattending meetings; no functional de-escalation mechanismremains
